Off Calendar Aug-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Marc Stimpert of Lunski & Stimpert LLP (415-426-3586) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, George Chang

Attorney, Robert K. Carrol of Arent Fox LLP (415-757-5500) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties, Tom an dTeresa Chang

Kevin Singer (415-848-2984) appears as court-appointed receiver

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS TOM CHANG AND TERESA CHANG'S Motion To Set Date For Valuation date of July 8, 2016 is denied. Based on the papers alone, the court is unable to discern which, if either, of the two very conflicting stories presented by the parties is more accurate. Because of this inability, the parties' retention of their damage claims against each other, and the lack of any impediment to or persuasive rationale why the defendants did not invoke the section 2000 procedure earlier (e.g., defendants contend that they knew about plaintiff's alleged misconduct before plaintiff filed this lawsuit), the court discerns no good cause to establish a valuation date different than the "default date" of February 11, 2015. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Off Calendar Aug-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARC STIMPERT OF LUNSKI & STIMPERT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-426-3586 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. APRIL S. GLATT & ATTY. RON CHAVEL OF APRIL S. GLATT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-650-573-9500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. AMY LAUGHLIN, TEL. NO. 1-650-343-3486 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. KYLE MONTES DE OCA OF ROECA HAAS HAGER LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-352-0980 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT TOM METAL SPECIALIST.

RULING - A R G U E D The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GEORGE CHANG'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver is Granted. The Court has considered the possibility of less-intrusive alternatives to receivership. [See Gold v. Gold Realty (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 791,807.] On May 11, 2015, a restraining order was issued with a detailed list of conditions, in an attempt to avoid the need for more serious measures. When that approach failed, On July 13, 2015 the Court spent several hours working out a stipulation and order, signed by all parties, wherein they committed to a new, supplemented set of conditions for management of the corporation until the resolution of the case. The very next day, Defendants procured a domestic violence restraining order from San Mateo Superior Court which conflicts with the provisions of both the May 11, 2015 and July 14, 2015 orders of this Court. In this circumstance, the court Court finds a receivership is necessary to prevent the very real possibility of harm to the corporation and its shareholders before this case is resolved. (See Corporations Code 1803.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
